captured_by: test-designer (goal 061 leg B) — expected.*.json derived INDEPENDENTLY of any parser (no uk_commons_register adapter exists at capture time), per docs/decisions/automation-policy.md: hand transcription from the raw fixture JSON + SAF mapping rules (Pass A) cross-checked cell-by-cell against a second, fresh-context model derivation (Pass B, spec+inputs only); zero divergences. A third mechanical pass (node stdlib re-derivation of every Silver/Gold cell from input.json per SAF §3.4/§3.5/§4/§5.1) also green, plus a raw-byte scan for the float-literal convention.
spec: docs/regimes/uk_commons_register.md (silver §4, details §5, mapping §5.1, value rules §3.4, owner §3.5, dates §3.6, confidence §6, fixture selection + version-scoped pins §7)

conformance_conventions.silver_shape: expected.silver.json is the serialization of Vec<pipeline StagingRow>: [{payload, confidence}] — same wrapper as us_house/us_senate. payload carries every docs/regimes/uk_commons_register.md §4 field EXCEPT confidence, which lives at the wrapper's top-level `confidence` (f32); no duplication inside payload. Exactly one row per document (§3.8 check 5).
conformance_conventions.confidence_literals: every fixture row scores 1.00 per SAF §6 (registration_date_raw present on all five; no sum_of_donors) and MUST be the JSON float literal 1.0 — never integer 1, which serde_json parses as a PosInt Number that deep-compares UNEQUAL to Float 1.0 (us_senate MANIFEST rule). No sub-1.0 image appears in this adapter's fixtures, so no f32→f64 cast literals here; if a future case scores below 1.00, pin the exact f64 image of the f32 (us_house 0.98 → 0.9800000190734863 precedent).
conformance_conventions.fields_raw_verbatim: silver `fields_raw` is the source `fields` array VERBATIM: `typeInfo` kept as null or {currencyCode}, `value` in its source JSON type (Decimal→string, Boolean→bool, Int→number, DateOnly→string, null), source order, human typos ('resigtered') and non-ASCII ('Gŵyl', '£') untouched. The five pinned documents carry NO `values` key on any field (the nested Donor[]/VisitLocation[] shape is not in these categories) — fields_raw therefore has no `values` key either; raw is sacred.
conformance_conventions.details_fields_flatten: details.fields is the §5 flattened Field shape {name, description, type, currency_code, value, values}: currency_code = typeInfo.currencyCode (null when typeInfo null — money ⇔ currency_code present, never the field name/type alone), value verbatim, values = explicit null on these flat documents (details-nulls convention below; the array-of-array-of-Field shape is reserved for Donor[]/VisitLocation[] categories, not fixtured here).
conformance_conventions.details_nulls: Optional details fields are serialized as explicit nulls (no skip_serializing_if) so the (uk_commons_register, interest) contract surface is fully visible in every candidate: parent_interest_id, registration_date, rectified_details, shareholding_threshold_raw, and every fields[].currency_code/value/values are null when absent.
conformance_conventions.money_strings: all Gold value bounds are decimal STRINGS end to end (invariant 7): '70000.00', '3400.00', '500.00'; open-ended = low set, high JSON null, currency 'GBP'.
conformance_conventions.interest_gold_shape: first `interest` record_type adapter: side/transaction_date/as_of_date NULL on every row (GoldCandidate::validate() requires nothing for Interest); notified_date = registrationDate (§3.6 — NEVER publishedDate); instrument_id NULL (invariant 3); fingerprint NULL (computed at promotion, Task 6).
conformance_conventions.extractor: uk_commons_register/api@1 (§4 — deterministic serde_json path; no LLM seam on this adapter's green path, SAF §6)

conformance_ids.rationale: API documents carry no ULIDs and conformance runs with pool: None, so filing/politician/regime ids cannot be resolved from a roster. These fixed Crockford-valid ULID constants are the contract the adapter must emit in conformance mode (production resolves real ids from Postgres). Prefix is 0GBR* — NOT 0UK*: 'U' is outside the Crockford base32 alphabet and would not parse. Filing ids embed the interest id digits for auditability (mirroring us_house DocID / us_senate UUID-group embedding); all pinned fixtures are version 0, so the version is not embedded — an update-case fixture ({id}@{n}, §2.5) would need its own constant.
conformance_ids.politician_key_note: keys are the numeric MNIS member.id as strings — the SAF §2.4 resolution input (exact id join, no name matching); pool-backed runs resolve via the politician external-id roster

builder_notes.input_bytes: input.json files are the RAW response bytes of GET /api/v1/Interests/{id} — byte-identical to the docs/regimes/uk_commons_register/evidence/ archives and to the SAF §7 pins (re-verified live at this capture, 5/5 sha match; this second session also CONFIRMS cross-session byte stability, closing that SAF §7 open question). Parse from raw bytes as UTF-8; no re-serialization.
builder_notes.id_threading: unlike us_house/us_senate pages, the document DOES print its own id (top-level `id`) — §3.8 check 1 still requires the pipeline to thread the requested id and reject a mismatch. version = updatedDates.length must equal the FilingRef version (§3.8 check 4); all fixtures are version 0.
builder_notes.conformance_registry: crates/pipeline/src/conformance.rs details_schema() has NO (uk_commons_register, interest) arm yet — the harness fails closed on it (checked at capture time). Builder leg: add the arm + snapshot-commit crates/pipeline/schemas/details/uk_commons_register.interest.json from the schemars type in crates/adapters/uk_commons_register/src/details.rs (seed `{}` placeholder first — schema-contracts bootstrap order), and ship a `conformance_entry` bin (constant name per the dispatcher contract) so `cargo run -p pipeline --bin conformance -- uk_commons_register` dispatches.
builder_notes.harness_blast_radius: verified at capture time: nothing globs across adapter fixture roots — e2e_local/promote/extraction/contract tests and the worker local bin are all scoped to fixtures_dir("us_house") or fixture_fake, and role evals hardcode us_house. The scaffold crate (Cargo.toml + empty lib.rs) keeps the crates/adapters/* workspace glob valid (us_house CI-red lesson, commit c6dceb7).
builder_notes.eol_pinning: .gitattributes already covers these paths (crates/adapters/*/fixtures/** -text, docs/regimes/*/evidence/** -text) — verify `git ls-files --eol` shows i/w agreement before any future push touching pinned bytes (evidence-archiving skill learning).
